commands.review_summary:
The PR adds an opt-in `--show-url-in-notes` flag for add/edit URL operations, mirrors/removes a managed notes line when opted in, preserves default EventKit URL behavior, and updates docs/help/tests.

Reproducibility: not applicable. for this PR as a patch review. The linked issue has user screenshots, and the PR body reports live macOS/EventKit proof for the proposed opt-in behavior.

target.title: fix: show reminder URLs in Reminders notes
target.body:
Closes #59.

Keeps `--url` in the public EventKit URL field and also mirrors it to one tool-owned `remindctl URL (managed): ...` notes line so the link is visible in Reminders.app. `edit --url` replaces that managed line, notes-only edits retain it, and `--clear-url` removes the EventKit value plus only the managed line while preserving authored notes.

This rewrites the contributor baseline to make the existing `--url` command satisfy the reported behavior directly; no additional opt-in flag is required. Authored `URL: ...` and plain `remindctl URL: ...` notes remain untouched.

## Proof

- `swift test --filter ReminderURLNoteMirrorTests`: 11 focused URL-mapping tests passed.
- `make check`: strict formatting/SwiftLint passed; 65 tests passed; RemindCore line coverage 91.1% (613/673, 90% required).
- `make build`: signed release CLI built successfully; built `add --help`/`edit --help` show the EventKit + Reminders notes contract.
- `make docs-site`: documentation site built successfully.
- Live macOS 26.5.2 / Swift 6.3.3 proof used one uniquely named disposable list/reminder: add and `info` returned the EventKit URL plus managed notes link; notes-only edit retained it; URL edit replaced it; `--clear-url` removed the URL and only the managed line; reminder and list were deleted; cleanup verified no test list remained.
- Codex autoreview: accepted and fixed one notes-only-edit finding; rerun found no code defects. A later generated-state P3 was rejected because the file is globally ignored, untracked, and absent from this PR diff.

No release, version, tag, or registry action included.
